scenes.phrase: You've paid Spotify more money every single year since 2023
And artists are getting paid less per stream than ever
Let me say that again — most insane sentence in the music industry
You, the listener, are paying more for Spotify today than ever in its 18-year history
Spotify raised its prices in 2023, 2024, 2025, and announced another hike for 2026
Same app, same songs, same skip button. Four price increases in four years.
Artists whose songs you press play on every day are watching their per-stream payout collapse
You pay more, they get less. Spotify keeps the difference.
If that sounds like a scam, it's because it is. A very polite one, with a green logo.

cmds.text: if that sounds like a scam...

scenes.phrase: So today we're going to do something nobody at Spotify wants you to do. Read the receipt.

cmds.text: nobody at Spotify wants you to do this.

scenes.phrase: Where money goes. Why artists quit. App worse. Podcasts. Fake numbers. Weapons.
This is not a hit piece. This is an audit. Where did your $11.99 actually go?
2021. Spotify Premium cost $9.99. It had cost $9.99 since 2014. Seven years, zero increases.
You pay $10, get every song ever recorded. Best deal in history of consumer media. Insane.
June 2023. First hike. $10.99. Spotify blamed market conditions — the real reason was simpler.
Wall Street demanded growth, so they started squeezing the ones they already had.
April 2024. Second hike. $11.99. They called it expansion of features.
Audiobooks you didn't ask for, added anyway. That's not a feature. That's a hostage.
July 2025. Third hike. Individual $12.99, Family $19.99, Duo $17.99 — a psychological trick.
2026. Fourth hike. $14.99. You are now paying 50% more than 3 years ago. Same app.
